text:
Unite × Impact × Bungee Gum (合体×衝撃×バンジーガム, Gōtai × Shōgeki × Banjī Gamu) is the 86 th episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime and the 8 th episode of the Greed Island final OVA series. It was released on DVD on April 28 th , 2004.

Overview

Gon wants Killua to continue holding the ball for him, and as the match unfolds, the trio of Gon, Killua, and Hisoka establishes a unified defense to successfully catch Razor's powerful strike. In the match's final moments, Gon collapses from exhaustion, but Hisoka steps up and uses his bungee gum to volley Razor's counterattack back, clinching their victory. Following the match, Gon and Razor discuss Ging, and after the group successfully obtains the card, they are contacted by Genthru.

Synopsis

Gon admits that he has known about Killua’s injured hands all along, leaving everyone in awe. He dismisses Tsezguerra’s recommendations, asserting that he will not play in the outfield and insisting that Killua must be the one to hold the dodgeball. Gon explains that with Killua holding the ball, he can focus more effectively and strike with full force. Hearing this, Killua feels a sense of relief. Gon’s words prompt Tsezguerra to withdraw his suggestion and step back, leaving the group to manage things their way.

Meanwhile, an energized Killua reminds Gon that they must reclaim the ball from him to defeat Razor. Gon gathers the group and quietly shares a strategy. Hisoka is thrilled by the plan, but Killua hesitates, clearly uncomfortable with the idea.

The match resumes, with Razor standing alone as he reabsorbs his Nen beasts, creating a massive aura around him. Hisoka reminds Gon that Razor is gathering the aura he had dispersed earlier, meaning his next strike will unleash his full strength. Gon gives a determined nod.

Razor channels all his immense aura into the ball in his hands, commenting that he hasn’t felt this good in a long time. As he releases the ball into the air and prepares to attack, Tsezguerra analyzes his stance and predicts that Razor aims for a volleyball-style strike. At the same moment, Gon, Killua, and Hisoka form a unified defensive stance, their auras radiating around them. As Razor jumps to spike the ball, seemingly aware of what the trio is planning, he unleashes his full strength, sending the ball hurtling toward them with immense force. Anticipating the strike, Gon catches the ball in his hands while Hisoka reinforces it with his aura, and Killua provides support to maintain their balance. The ball spins rapidly in Gon’s hands, radiating with powerful energy, and moments later, the trio is engulfed in a cloud of smoke. When the smoke clears, Killua and Hisoka are separated from Gon by the impact, but Gon successfully secures the ball. The others cheer in celebration, and even Tsezguerra, surprised by their achievement, acknowledges and praises their efforts while Biscuit sighs in relief.

On the other side of the field, Razor also praises the trio, particularly their ability to catch his powerful attack. He acknowledges that their combined talent overcame his strength and explains that each of them played a crucial role. Gon enveloped his hands with an immense amount of aura, allowing him to catch the ball without hesitation. Hisoka's precision in wrapping the ball with his aura upon impact ensured that it wouldn’t deflect unpredictably. However, Razor emphasizes that Killua played the most critical role, standing between them to soften the impact and provide support. Killua's swift manipulation of his aura, balancing offense and defense, was essential in preventing them from being severely injured by the force of the strike. Razor also notes that Killua’s precise footwork was key to their successful defense, further commending his exceptional talent. Even Biscuit is stunned by Gon and Killua's abilities, remarking in disbelief that the level they have reached at their age was something she only achieved in her late twenties. Razor devises his next strategy, planning to attack them before they can unite while preparing himself to counter Gon’s assault.

Meanwhile, Gon channels a massive amount of aura, shocking everyone, including Razor by revealing that he hasn’t unleashed his full power during his previous attempt. Upon witnessing Gon’s immense aura, Razor confidently acknowledges that Gon is undeniably Ging’s son. Gon tells Killua that he is giving it his all, and Killua agrees, warning him that he should, or else he will pulverize him. Gon strikes the ball with his Janken Rock. Razor anticipates the ball coming toward him and considers a way to catch it without getting blown out of the field. Changing his posture, he releases his aura. Rodriot, familiar with the stance, shouts to Razor that his plan won’t work because Hisoka will simply catch the ball in the air with his bungee gum. Razor ignores him and lets out a small grin. Razor hits the ball directly toward the trio instead of parrying it through the air. Tsezguerra, who is watching, advises Gon to dodge it; however, Razor is confident that Gon won’t evade the attack, insisting that he is not the type to win that way. As the ball approaches Gon, it seems he will be hit, but he suddenly collapses to the ground, fainting from exhaustion. Given the amount of aura Gon has used, it’s clear he has pushed himself to his limits, according to Tsezguerra. He adds that Gon should be proud of his efforts regardless of the outcome. Much to his surprise, Hisoka steps up, declaring that this is exactly how Gon wants it. He quickly creates a net-like structure with his bungee gum. As the ball reaches him, he catches it effortlessly, allowing it to stretch further back. With a powerful motion, he volleys the ball back toward Razor using the force from his bungee gum.

Razor is prepared and firmly states that the situation has now turned into an exchange of rallies, ready to receive and return Hisoka’s attack. As the ball strikes Razor's arms, he attempts to send it back to his opponents but quickly realizes that the ball isn’t budging. Hisoka explains that his Bungee Gum possesses the properties of both rubber and gum. Struggling to free the ball from his grip, Razor finds it stuck in his hand. The force of Hisoka’s counterattack pushes him back, ultimately forcing him out of the field and tearing his shoe in the process. Everyone was shocked, and the referee officially announced the match was over and that Razor was out after catching the ball outside of the field and declared Gon’s team winning the match. As the group celebrates, Gon awakes, seemingly unaware of the events that happened when he was unconscious. Razor calmly accepts his defeat, and Killua tells Gon that Hisoka stole the limelight, through Hisoka acknowledged everyone's effort.

Razor approaches Gon, acknowledging his defeat and agreeing to leave the island. He also tells Gon that he will answer all his questions regarding Ging. The two then step away from the group to have a private conversation. Razor quickly tells Gon that Ging is not on the island and doesn’t know where he is, which Gon anticipates; however, Razor tells him that he can share old stories of Ging with Gon, to which Gon accepts. In their conversation, Razor reveals that he is also a criminal, was caught by Ging, and was sent to prison. Upon serving his sentence, he is hired by Ging and brought to Greed Island, and Ging is the only person who believes in him and that he can be saved. As Gon and Razor continue to discuss Ging, they delve into Greed Island and Razor's involvement. 

After their talks, the pirates leave the area, and the group arrives at the lighthouse, where they watch a certain event in the game progress with a female NPC. Once her speech concludes, she disappears and transforms into a card, allowing the group to finally obtain the "Plot of the Beach" card. They make copies of the card for each member and decide that Gon, Killua, and Biscuit will keep the original.

Hisoka bids farewell, refusing to take anything from the group, explaining that he has no interest in collecting cards. He tells them that if they ever need his help, they can contact him, and he will use either Accompany or Magnetic to reach them. After Hisoka leaves, Killua realizes Hisoka lied, stating that he already knew about the spell card before meeting them and is sure that he is hiding something as the rest of the group continues to discuss among themselves.

Later, Tsezguerra informs the group that they have joined forces with Goreinu in anticipation of facing Genthru and his group in the near future. He outlines the disadvantages they face against Genthru's team, highlighting Genthru's unethical tactics in the game. Just then, Tsezguerra receives a call from Genthru.
